meta.description: Awwwards jury profiling — composition, preferences, scoring tendencies, biases, and evaluation behavior

juryComposition.activePerSite: 18-60 jury members vote per nominated site (minimum 18 required for valid scoring)

memberTypes.bias: Design quality, visual originality, brand storytelling
memberTypes.evaluationFocus: Does it push creative boundaries? Is the design language cohesive?
Is this technically novel? Does DevTools inspection reveal quality?
Is the navigation clear? Does the design serve the user?
memberTypes.concern: Heavy WebGL sites often score lower from UX-focused jury members

memberTypes.bias: Content quality, brand communication, business impact
memberTypes.evaluationFocus: Does this effectively communicate? Does the tech serve the message?
Does this advance the medium? Can the community learn from it?

regions.tendency: High design standards, appreciation for restraint and typography
Technical innovation valued, scale of experience appreciated
Precision and attention to detail highly valued
Creative boldness appreciated, emerging scene enthusiasm

geographicDistribution.implication: European jury members form the largest block. European design sensibility (restraint, typography, negative space, editorial approach) tends to score well. Heavy, flashy WebGL without design purpose can be penalized by this cohort.

scoreDistribution.8to85: ~20% of nominees — strong SOTD, SOTM potential

scoringTendencies.generosityLevel: Moderate — jury is neither overly generous nor harsh. Scores above 9.0 are genuinely rare and indicate exceptional quality.

outlierRemoval.process: The 3 highest and 3 lowest scores are removed before averaging
outlierRemoval.purpose: Prevents any single jury member from disproportionately influencing the final score
outlierRemoval.implication: One bad review won't tank you, but one great review won't save you either. You need CONSENSUS of quality.

categoryBehavior.design: Most generous category — beautiful sites often get 0.3-0.5 points higher in design than other categories
categoryBehavior.usability: Strictest category — even beautiful sites get penalized for confusing navigation, broken mobile, or poor accessibility
categoryBehavior.creativity: Most variable — jury members disagree most about what constitutes 'creative.' WebGL innovations score high, but so can minimal sites with clever concepts.
categoryBehavior.content: Most overlooked — jury spends less time evaluating content depth. But projects with strong content consistently score higher overall.

triggers: Every single pixel is intentional — zero accidental elements
Color palette is unique and memorable
Typography is exceptional (custom typeface or masterful use of existing)
Composition breaks conventions while maintaining harmony
The design has a recognizable 'voice' — you'd know it if you saw it again
Animation design is as considered as static design
design10.frequency: Rare — maybe 5-10 jury members per year give a 10 for design

triggers: Navigation is instantly intuitive despite unconventional presentation
Loads fast, performs smoothly on all devices
Mobile experience is excellent, not just acceptable
Accessibility considered (keyboard, screen reader, reduced motion)
User never feels lost or frustrated
The UX innovations serve the user, not just the designer's ego
usability10.frequency: Very rare for immersive sites — Pangram Pangram is the exception

triggers: Something genuinely NEW — technique, concept, or approach never seen before
The innovation changes how you think about what's possible in a browser
Creative use of technology to tell a story or evoke emotion
The site would be remembered 5 years later
It inspires other creators to push their own work
creativity10.frequency: Most common 10 — when jury sees something truly novel, they want to reward it

triggers: The content IS the experience — not just text filling a template
Educational value — you learn something meaningful
Storytelling that uses the web medium to enhance narrative
Writing quality is exceptional
The content justifies the technical investment
content10.frequency: Rare — most sites don't invest enough in content to earn a 10

below7.impact: Immediate score reduction across ALL categories (not just usability)
below7.severity: High — a single 5-second lag spike during jury evaluation can drop score by 0.5+
below7.prevention: Adaptive quality, performance profiling, frame budget monitoring

below7.impact: -0.5 to -1.0 overall (significant because ~40% of jury tests on mobile)
below7.prevention: Dedicated mobile testing, responsive design, mobile-specific optimizations

below7.severity: Medium-High — many jury members evaluate in open offices or public spaces
below7.prevention: ALWAYS mute by default. Elegant opt-in.

below7.impact: -0.2 to -0.3 — jury perceives the site as unfinished or poorly optimized
below7.prevention: Engaging preloader that sets the tone for the experience

below7.issue: Poor accessibility (no keyboard nav, screen reader broken)
below7.prevention: Basic accessibility: keyboard navigation, screen reader content, reduced motion support
Clear progress indication, visible navigation, ability to skip sections

below7.impact: -0.2 to -0.3 in design and creativity (seen as 'showing off' rather than 'designing')
below7.prevention: Every effect must serve the narrative or enhance comprehension

below7.impact: -0.3 to -0.5 — jury has limited patience, especially during evaluation of multiple sites
below7.prevention: Progressive loading, engaging preloader, critical-path optimization
Zero console errors in production. No console.log. No deprecation warnings.
If hijacking scroll, the payoff must be worth the loss of native behavior

positiveBiases.reason: Dark themes are perceived as 'premium' and photograph well in screenshots/social media
positiveBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Positive — black hole theme is inherently dark

positiveBiases.reason: Technical impressiveness is viscerally felt. 'How did they do that?' drives high creativity scores.
positiveBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Very positive — GPGPU + raymarching will impress

positiveBiases.reason: Largest jury block is European. European design values (restraint, typography, whitespace) are overrepresented in scoring.
positiveBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Neutral — depends on execution. If Event Horizon has European-level design refinement alongside WebGL spectacle, it hits both notes.

positiveBiases.reason: Jury appreciates restraint. 'Knowing what to leave out' is valued.
positiveBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Relevant — the emptiness of space IS negative space. Use it.

positiveBiases.reason: Jury roots for the underdog. A small team creating something extraordinary gets extra goodwill.
positiveBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Positive if marketed as small-team project

positiveBiases.reason: Jury increasingly expects sound in immersive experiences. Sites with sound consistently score 0.1-0.3 higher in creativity.
positiveBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Must have sound — and it must be physics-based and integrated

negativeBiases.reason: Jury has seen too many 'look at my WebGL' sites with no story. The question 'but what is it ABOUT?' kills scores.
negativeBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Risk area — must have strong astrophysics narrative, not just cool black hole

negativeBiases.bias: Heavy sites that don't respect user's device
negativeBiases.reason: Jury members on average laptops get frustrated by GPU-heavy sites that make their fan spin
negativeBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Critical risk — GPGPU + raymarching is heavy. MUST have adaptive quality.

negativeBiases.reason: Jury split on these topics. Some see innovation, others see hype.
negativeBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Not applicable — astrophysics theme is universally appealing

negativeBiases.reason: Jury evaluates many sites per session. 10+ minute experiences test patience.
negativeBiases.eventHorizonImpact: Keep the core experience to 3-5 minutes. Offer exploration beyond that as optional.

evaluationProcess: First impression: landing page, loading experience, initial visual impact (first 10 seconds)
Exploration: scroll through experience, test interactions, check navigation (1-3 minutes)
Mobile check: ~40% of jury checks mobile view (either on phone or DevTools responsive mode)
Technical inspection: developer-focused jury members open DevTools (20-30% of jury)
Score assignment: based on overall impression, specific highs/lows noted during exploration

criticalMoments.what: Loading experience → first visual. If this is impressive, the jury member is primed to score generously. If it's slow/broken, they're primed to find more problems.
How does the site respond to scroll? Smooth? Purposeful? If the first scroll doesn't reward the user, engagement drops.
The single strongest visual/interactive moment. This is what the jury member will REMEMBER when assigning the creativity score.
If mobile is broken or laggy, usability score drops significantly. If mobile is surprisingly good, it raises all scores.
How does the experience end? A strong ending leaves a positive last impression. A sudden stop or broken end leaves a negative one.

votingMechanics.minimumVotes: 18 jury members must vote for a valid score
votingMechanics.highVoteCounts: 50+ indicates strong jury engagement (positive signal for SOTY consideration)
votingMechanics.outlierRemoval: Top 3 and bottom 3 scores removed before averaging
votingMechanics.publicVoteWeight: Exists but minimal compared to jury scores. Public vote can break ties between close jury scores.
votingMechanics.developerAwardJury: Subset of jury with verified development expertise. ~50-80 members. Vote separately on technical innovation.

strategicInsights.insight: The first 10 seconds determine the trajectory of all scores
strategicInsights.action: Event Horizon's loading experience → first reveal must be STUNNING. Consider: stars materializing in the void → camera pulling back to reveal the black hole in the distance → text fading in with title. All within 3-5 seconds of first meaningful content.

strategicInsights.insight: Jury fatigue is real — they evaluate multiple sites per session
strategicInsights.action: Make the experience impactful but not exhausting. 3-5 minute core journey with optional deep exploration. Respect the jury's time.

strategicInsights.insight: Mobile check happens more than you think
strategicInsights.action: Assume EVERY jury member will check mobile. The mobile experience must stand on its own, not just be a degraded desktop.

strategicInsights.insight: Developer jury members WILL open DevTools
strategicInsights.action: Zero console errors. Clean network tab. Reasonable bundle sizes. Well-structured source code. Consider adding a hidden console message: 'Interested in the technical details? Read our writeup at [URL]'

strategicInsights.insight: The description you write for submission influences scoring
strategicInsights.action: Write a compelling 2-3 sentence description that explains the innovation WITHOUT being technical. Something like: 'An interactive journey to the edge of spacetime. Experience what happens when nothing — not even light — can escape.'
Share on Twitter/X, LinkedIn, and creative communities (Codrops, Creative Boom, etc.) after nomination. More visibility → more jury members vote → higher chance of winning.
Publish a detailed blog post about the GPGPU and raymarching techniques BEFORE submitting to Awwwards. Share it with the developer community. Jury members will reference it.

strengthsForThisJury: Dark/cosmic theme aligns with positive biases
WebGL innovation will excite developer jury members
Universal theme (black holes) appeals across cultural backgrounds
Small team narrative resonates with indie bias
Sound design opportunity (physics-based) will impress if well-executed
risksForThisJury: Heavy GPU load could lag on jury members' average hardware → adaptive quality MANDATORY
Risk of 'tech demo without narrative' perception → content/story must be strong
Scroll hijacking could frustrate UX-focused jury → consider escape hatch
Mobile could be weak point → dedicated mobile experience needed
European jury might want more design restraint alongside spectacle → balance explosion with calm
eventHorizonJuryMatchAnalysis.overallJuryCompatibility: High — Event Horizon's theme and innovation align well with jury preferences. The risks are manageable with intentional design decisions.
